metadata.rationale:
1) Identify the values: output is the word "four"; expected is the numeral "4".
2) Semantic check: "four" and "4" represent the same integer value (4), so they are semantically equivalent.
3) Format/type check: one is a spelled-out word, the other a digit character; they are not exact string-equals.
4) Typical evaluation criteria: if the evaluator normalizes numeric words to digits or compares numeric value, this is a full match; if the evaluator requires exact token/string equality, this is a non-match.
5) Choose a single scalar score reflecting high semantic equivalence but noting the formatting mismatch: assign 0.9 (high but not perfect to reflect a formatting/type difference).
